script_frameworks.description: User-generated content style video with a strong hook, problem-solution body, social proof, and clear call to action. Feels organic and relatable.

sections.purpose: Stop the scroll. Open with a bold, relatable statement or question that speaks directly to the target audience.
guidelines: Speak directly to camera
Use 'you' language or first-person revelation
Must be understood without sound (add text overlay)
Pattern interrupt — say something unexpected
example_lines: I found the best-kept secret in Malta and I need to tell you about it
POV: You finally booked that spa day you've been putting off for months
Nobody talks about this but every woman in Malta needs to hear it

sections.purpose: Agitate the pain point. Make the viewer feel seen and understood.
guidelines: Describe a specific, relatable frustration
Use emotional language
Keep it conversational — like talking to a friend
example_lines: You're exhausted, your back is killing you, and the last time you did something for yourself was... you can't even remember
You've been thinking about it for months but you keep putting everyone else first

sections.purpose: Introduce the brand/offer as the answer. Show or describe the experience.
guidelines: Transition naturally from problem to solution
Show the experience visually if possible (B-roll of spa, treatment room)
Mention the brand name naturally
Highlight one key benefit
example_lines: That's why I booked a spa day at Carisma Spa — and honestly, it changed my whole week
Carisma Aesthetics made the whole process so easy — from consultation to results, I felt completely looked after

sections.purpose: Build trust and credibility. Reinforce the solution with evidence.
guidelines: Share personal result or transformation
Mention specific details (staff, atmosphere, results)
Can reference reviews or popularity
example_lines: The therapists were incredible, the facilities are stunning, and I've already booked my next visit
I've had so many compliments and honestly, nobody can even tell — it's just me but refreshed

sections.purpose: Drive action. Tell the viewer exactly what to do next.
guidelines: Be specific and direct
Add urgency if applicable
Include text overlay with the CTA
End with confidence
example_lines: Link in bio to book — trust me, you won't regret it
Tap the link below and book your free consultation today
They've got limited spots this month so don't wait — book now

script_frameworks.description: Brand founder or lead practitioner speaks directly to camera, establishing authority and trust while delivering value. Professional yet personable.

sections.purpose: Capture attention with a bold claim, insider knowledge, or myth-busting statement.
guidelines: Speak with authority and confidence
Open with a surprising fact or misconception
Establish credibility immediately (setting, attire, demeanour)
Text overlay with key statement
example_lines: As someone who's been in the wellness industry for over 10 years, here's what most people get wrong
I'm the founder of Carisma Aesthetics and there's one thing I wish every woman in Malta knew

sections.purpose: Establish why the viewer should listen. Share credentials, experience, or unique perspective.
guidelines: Mention relevant experience or qualifications naturally
Show the environment (spa, clinic) in background
Be genuine — not boastful
Connect credentials to viewer benefit
example_lines: We've helped thousands of women across Malta feel their best — and we do it differently
After years of training and treating hundreds of clients, I developed an approach that focuses on natural results

sections.purpose: Deliver genuine value. Educate, inform, or reveal something useful that builds trust.
guidelines: Share a genuine tip, insight, or behind-the-scenes look
Be generous with information — this builds trust
Show passion and expertise
Can include a walkthrough of the space or process
Break down what makes your approach different
example_lines: Here's what makes our spa day different: we don't rush you. From the moment you walk in, it's your time. Thermal pools, steam rooms, a full 60-minute massage, and a healthy lunch — all in one visit
When clients come to us for Botox, we start with a full facial assessment. I'd never recommend something you don't need. It's about enhancing what you already have

sections.purpose: Invite the viewer to take action with a warm, confident close.
guidelines: Keep it invitational rather than pushy
Reflect the brand's tone
Mention the specific next step
Text overlay with CTA
example_lines: If this sounds like what you've been looking for, the link's right there — come see us
Book your free consultation through the link below — I'd love to help you feel your best

script_frameworks.description: Shows a transformation journey from before treatment to after results. Must comply with advertising standards — focus on realistic, natural results.
compliance_notes: Never use side-by-side before/after comparisons that could be misleading
Ensure consistent lighting and angles in before and after footage
Do not make exaggerated claims about results
Must have client consent for any footage used

sections.purpose: Tease the transformation to create curiosity. Do not reveal the result immediately.
guidelines: Create anticipation without spoiling the result
Use intrigue-based hooks
Text overlay to reinforce the hook
example_lines: Watch what happened when she finally booked that consultation she'd been thinking about
She was nervous, but the results speak for themselves
This is what natural-looking fillers actually look like

sections.purpose: Show the starting point. Be respectful and positive — never shame or focus on flaws.
guidelines: Show the client in natural lighting
Frame it positively — she's already beautiful, this is about enhancement
Can include a voiceover of the client sharing her goals
Never use negative or shaming language
example_lines: She came to us wanting a subtle lip enhancement — nothing dramatic, just a little more definition
Before: she felt her face looked tired even after a good night's sleep

sections.purpose: Reveal the results. Focus on the client's reaction and confidence, not just the physical change.
guidelines: Show genuine client reaction if possible
Use similar lighting and angles to the before
Focus on how she feels, not just how she looks
Keep it natural and authentic
example_lines: After: subtle, natural, and she couldn't stop smiling
Two weeks later — refreshed, confident, and still completely herself

sections.purpose: Explain the treatment briefly. Build confidence by showing expertise and care.
guidelines: Mention the treatment type and approach
Emphasise the consultation-first process
Highlight safety and expertise
Keep it accessible — avoid jargon
example_lines: We used a premium hyaluronic acid filler, applied with precision after a thorough consultation. The whole process took under 30 minutes
Our practitioner designed a personalised plan focused on natural enhancement — nothing overdone

sections.purpose: Drive consultation bookings. Emphasise the free, no-obligation consultation.
guidelines: Low-commitment CTA — consultation, not treatment
Reassure nervous prospects
Text overlay with CTA and disclaimer
example_lines: Curious? Book your free consultation — no commitment, just a chat about what's possible for you
Tap the link to book your free assessment. Individual results may vary.

script_frameworks.description: A real client shares her experience and recommendation. Authentic, emotional, and trust-building.

sections.purpose: The client opens with a strong, emotional statement that grabs attention.
guidelines: Start mid-story for authenticity — not from the beginning
Use genuine, unscripted-feeling language
Text overlay with the key quote
Should feel like a real person, not an actor
example_lines: Honestly? It was the best thing I've ever done for myself
I was so nervous but I'm so glad I went through with it
I walked out feeling like a completely different person

sections.purpose: The client tells her story — why she went, what the experience was like, and how she felt.
guidelines: Let the client speak naturally
Include specific details that feel authentic
Show emotion — this is where trust is built
Can intercut with B-roll of the spa or clinic
example_lines: I'd been putting it off for ages. My friend kept telling me to try Carisma Spa and finally I went. From the moment I walked in, I just felt... taken care of. The staff were so warm, the whole place was beautiful, and that massage — I actually fell asleep!

I'd wanted fillers for years but I was terrified of looking fake. My practitioner at Carisma spent 30 minutes just talking to me first, understanding what I wanted. She was so honest — she actually talked me out of one area and I'm so grateful for that

sections.purpose: The client gives her personal recommendation. Direct and heartfelt.
guidelines: Should feel like genuine advice to a friend
Mention who she'd recommend it to
Express genuine enthusiasm
example_lines: If you've been thinking about it, just go. I promise you won't regret it
I tell every single one of my friends — if you want someone you can trust, go to Carisma

sections.purpose: Brand-added CTA overlay on the final frames of the testimonial.
guidelines: Text overlay with booking CTA and URL
Include brand logo
Can include a limited-time offer if applicable
Keep the testimonial audio running underneath if possible
example_lines: Book your experience today — link below
Free consultation — tap to book now
Limited spots available — book this week

script_frameworks.description: Single-image ad designed for feed and story placements. Clean, branded, and focused on a specific offer with a clear CTA.

elements.purpose: Primary attention-grabbing text. Short, benefit-driven, and readable at small size.
guidelines: Maximum 6-8 words
Use sentence case
Focus on benefit or offer, not brand name
Must be legible at mobile size

elements.purpose: Supporting detail — price, included items, or urgency.
guidelines: Maximum 15 words
Include price or key detail
Complement the headline, don't repeat it
examples: Full spa day with massage and lunch — just €89

elements.purpose: High-quality hero image that conveys the experience or result.
guidelines: Professional photography preferred
Warm, inviting tones for spa; clean, clinical-but-warm for aesthetics
Feature real people where possible
Leave space for text overlays (rule of thirds)

elements.purpose: Clear action button or text CTA.
guidelines: Use contrasting colour for visibility
Action-oriented text: Book Now, Learn More, Get Yours
Positioned in lower third of the image
Position in corner — typically top-right or bottom-right
Use white or brand-colour version depending on background
Should not dominate the creative
